meaning: vt.供应,供给,使适应,调节,和解,向...提供,容纳,调和;vi.适应
examples: There is usually room to accommodate up to 80 visitors.
The school was not big enough to accommodate all the children.
The roads are built to accommodate gradual temperature changes.
That meant he had to keep hopping around the table to accommodate the diners.
Sometimes to accommodate cultural and individual needs, the courses are arranged for evenings and weekends.
